title: Periballia minuta
intro: Periballia minuta, also known as 'Lesser Ball Cactus', is a small, spineless cactus species native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Periballia minuta include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Periballia minuta is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as teas and salves.
